children.heading: Expedited licensing process for closed-loop pumped storage projects
children.label: a Expedited licensing process for closed-loop pumped storage projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: As provided in this section, the Commission may issue and amend licenses, as appropriate, for closed-loop pumped storage projects.

children.heading: Rule
children.label: 2 Rule
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Commission shall issue a rule establishing an expedited process for issuing and amending licenses for closed-loop pumped storage projects under this section.October 23, 2018

children.heading: Interagency task force
children.label: 3 Interagency task force
children.text: In establishing the expedited process under this section, the Commission shall convene an interagency task force, with appropriate Federal and State agencies and Indian tribes represented, to coordinate the regulatory processes associated with the authorizations required to construct and operate closed-loop pumped storage projects.

children.heading: Length of process
children.label: 4 Length of process
children.text: The Commission shall seek to ensure that the expedited process under this section will result in final decision on an application for a license by not later than 2 years after receipt of a completed application for such license.

children.heading: Dam safety
children.label: b Dam safety
children.text: Before issuing any license for a closed-loop pumped storage project, the Commission shall assess the safety of existing dams and other structures related to the project (including possible consequences associated with failure of such structures).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In issuing or amending a license for a closed-loop pumped storage project pursuant to the expedited process established under this section, the Commission may grant an exception from any other requirement of this subchapter with respect to any part of the closed-loop pumped storage project (not including any dam or other impoundment).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: In granting an exception under paragraph (1), the Commission shall consult with the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, the National Marine Fisheries Service, and the State agency exercising administration over the fish and wildlife resources of the State in which the closed-loop pumped storage project is or will be located, in the manner provided by the Fish and Wildlife Coordination Act ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 661

children.label: A
children.text: such terms and conditions as the United States Fish and Wildlife Service, the National Marine Fisheries Service, and the State agency described in paragraph (2) each determine are appropriate to prevent loss of, or damage to, fish and wildlife resources and to otherwise carry out the purposes of the Fish and Wildlife Coordination Act; and

children.label: B
children.text: such terms and conditions as the Commission deems appropriate to ensure that such closed-loop pumped storage project continues to comply with the provisions of this section and terms and conditions included in any such exception.

children.heading: Fees
children.label: 4 Fees
children.text: The Commission, in addition to the requirements of , shall establish fees which shall be paid by an applicant for a license for a closed-loop pumped storage project that is required to meet terms and conditions set by fish and wildlife agencies under paragraph (3). Such fees shall be adequate to reimburse the fish and wildlife agencies referred to in paragraph (3) for any reasonable costs incurred in connection with any studies or other reviews carried out by such agencies for purposes of compliance with this section. The fees shall, subject to annual appropriations Acts, be transferred to such agencies by the Commission for use solely for purposes of carrying out such studies and shall remain available until expended.section 803(e) of this title
add entities as joint permittees following issuance of a preliminary permit; and
transfer a license in part to one or more nonmunicipal entities as co-licensees with a municipality, if the municipality retains majority ownership of the project for which the license was issued.
be considered to be ex parte communications under Commission rules; or

preclude an agency from participating in a licensing proceeding under this subchapter, providing that any agency participating as a party in a licensing proceeding under this subchapter shall, to the extent practicable, demonstrate a separation of staff cooperating with the Commission under the National Environmental Policy Act ( et seq.) and staff participating in the applicable proceeding under this subchapter.142 U.S.C. 4321So in original. Probably should be followed by “of 1969”.1

children.heading: Developing abandoned mines for pumped storage
children.label: f Developing abandoned mines for pumped storage

children.heading: Workshop
children.label: 1 Workshop
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Commission shall hold a workshop to explore potential opportunities for development of closed-loop pumped storage projects at abandoned mine sites.October 23, 2018

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: 2 Guidance
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Commission shall issue guidance to assist applicants for licenses or preliminary permits for closed-loop pumped storage projects at abandoned mine sites.October 23, 2018

children.heading: Qualifying criteria for closed-loop pumped storage projects
children.label: g Qualifying criteria for closed-loop pumped storage projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall establish criteria that a pumped storage project shall meet in order to qualify as a closed-loop pumped storage project eligible for the expedited process established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: cause little to no change to existing surface and ground water flows and uses; and

children.label: B
children.text: is unlikely to adversely affect species listed as a threatened species or endangered species under the Endangered Species Act of 1973 [ et seq.].16 U.S.C. 1531

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: h Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this section affects any authority of the Commission to license a closed-loop pumped storage project under this subchapter.
